articles.title: ECB to hold the line at this week's meeting - Goldman Sachs
articles.expand: ECB policymakers have made it quite clear that they are valuing optionality as we approach the next policy decision this week. And markets are also listening, paring back on rate hike pricing with odds of such a move now seen at ~20% only. Those odds do jump back up to ~63% by the time we get to the June meeting though. And for the year itself, tra...

articles.title: BoJ preview: no change expected amid the US-Iran uncertainty
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to keep the policy rate steady at 0.75% tomorrow. The central bank will also release the quarterly outlook report where inflation is expected to be revised higher, while growth lower due to the US-Iran war. There were some speculations a couple of weeks ago that the BoJ could have hiked already in April, but the...

articles.title: Australian Dollar advances as surging energy prices increased RBA rate hike bets
articles.expand: AUD/USD extends its gains for the second consecutive day, trading around 0.7160 during the early European hours on Monday.

articles.title: EUR/USD Forex Signal: Volatility Expected Ahead of Fed and ECB Decisions - 27 April 2026
articles.expand: The EUR/USD exchange rate held steady at 1.1700 on Monday morning ahead of a busy week in which the US and Europe will release their numbers and the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Federal Reserve will deliver their interest rate decisions.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Market eyes June BoJ hike signal – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur says USD/JPY traders are focused on the upcoming Bank of Japan (BoJ) meeting, where a rate hike is seen as very unlikely and largely priced out versus three weeks ago.

articles.title: GBP/JPY edges up to near 215.70 in countdown to BoJ policy
articles.expand: The GBP/JPY pair ticks up to near 215.70 during the early European trade on Monday. The pair edges higher as the Japanese Yen (JPY) underperforms its major currency peers amid uncertainty surrounding the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) monetary policy, which will be announced on Tuesday.

articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION As is typical for a Monday, the European session calendar is empty. The markets will likely continue to consolidate as we await some breakthrough in the current US-Iran stalemate. Iran proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the lifting of the US blockade, with nuclear talks to follow at a later stage. We will see i...

articles.title: EUR/GBP steadies above 0.8650, traders await BoE and ECB rate decisions amid Middle East uncertainty
articles.expand: The EUR/GBP cross holds steady around 0.8660 during the early European trading hours on Monday. Markets turn cautious ahead of the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Bank of England (BoE) interest rate decisions on Thursday.

articles.title: Germany May GfK consumer sentiment -33.3 vs -29.3 expected
articles.expand: Prior -28.0; revised to -28.1 The German consumer climate worsens to a three-year low as the fallout from the Middle East conflict continues to take a toll on Europe's largest economy. In particular, higher energy prices and in turn higher inflation is translating into a major worry for German households. And that is reflected by a nosedive in inco...

articles.title: AI could quickly become the Fed’s new problem
articles.expand: A strong economy and a flimsy labor market don’t typically go hand in hand. For the Fed and other central banks, the playbook has long been straightforward: weak hiring calls for lower interest rates, while an overheating economy and inflation above target justify tighter policy.

articles.title: Chart alert: AUD/USD kickstarts fresh bullish impulsive sequence above 0.7090 key support
articles.expand: AUD/USD is showing renewed bullish momentum after rebounding above 0.7090, supported by improving risk sentiment and easing geopolitical tensions around the Strait of Hormuz. The pair continues to track global equities closely, with correlation at elevated levels. A break above 0.7211 could trigger a fresh rally toward 0.7300, while failure to hold...

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD turns positive for the second straight day
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair attracts some dip-buyers in the vicinity of the 1.3500 psychological mark and climbs to over a one-week top during the Asian session on Monday. Spot prices currently trade just below mid-1.3500s, up 0.10% for the day, and seem poised to appreciate further.

articles.title: AUD/JPY gathers strength above 114.00 on Iran truce proposal
articles.expand: The AUD/JPY cross gathers strength to around 114.15 during the early European session on Monday. Reports that Iran gave the US a proposal for reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war improve market sentiment, supporting the riskier currencies such as the Australian Dollar (AUD).

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Seems vulnerable near 1.3660; 200-hour EMA holds the key for bulls
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts some sellers following a modest Asian session uptick to the 1.3680 area on Monday, though it lacks follow-through selling.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bulls likely to target 1.3600 as USD weakens on US-Iran peace hopes
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair attracts some dip-buyers in the vicinity of the 1.3500 psychological mark and climbs to over a one-week top during the Asian session on Monday. Spot prices currently trade just below mid-1.3500s, up 0.10% for the day, and seem poised to appreciate further.

articles.title: WTI drifts higher above $93.50 on Hormuz uncertainty
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the US crude oil benchmark, is trading around $93.65 during the Asian trading hours on Monday. The WTI price edges higher as transit through the Strait of Hormuz remains severely restricted and peace talks between the United States and Iran have stalled.

articles.title: AUD/JPY Eyes Breakout Toward 120 as BoJ Decision and Australia CPI Set Up High-Stakes Week
articles.expand: AUD/JPY is setting up for a potential upside breakout toward 120 psychological level, with the next move likely to be decided by a high-stakes combination of BoJ policy and Australian inflation data. articles.title: US Dollar Index declines below 98.50 as Iran offers US deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 98.45 during the Asian trading hours on Monday. The DXY edges lower after reports that Iran offered the US a proposal for reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Advances to 0.7170 as bulls await range breakout on softer USD
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair turns positive for the second consecutive day following a modest dip on Monday and climbs to a three-day high, around the 0.7170 region during the Asian session.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar advances as oil prices gain on stalled US–Iran peace talks
articles.expand: USD/CAD remains subdued for the second successive day, trading around 1.3660 during the Asian hours on Monday. The pair loses ground as the commodity-linked Canadian Dollar (CAD) receives support from higher oil prices, given Canada’s status as the largest crude exporter to the United States (US).

articles.title: Japanese Yen holds steady as BoJ and Fed rate decisions in focus
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair trades on a flat note near 159.50 during the early Asian session on Monday. Traders prefer to wait on the sidelines ahead of the key interest rate decisions from both the Bank of Japan (BoJ) and the US Federal Reserve (Fed).

articles.title: Markets Weekly Outlook - Can earnings outweigh geopolitical headwinds & Central Bank decisions?
articles.expand: Global markets face a pivotal week defined by surging oil prices amid US-Iran tensions, record-high US equities driven by tech and earnings optimism, and critical central bank decisions. The Fed, ECB, and BoE grapple with the "balancing problem" of inflation vs. growth, while the BoJ remains the 'wild card' for a surprise rate hike.

articles.expand: Eurozone business activity slipped back into contraction in April, with the Flash Composite PMI dropping from 50.7 to 48.6, a 17-month low. The downturn was driven primarily by a sharp deterioration in the services sector, where activity fell from 50.2 to 47.4, the weakest level in over five years.
